authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Rancho San Rafael Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Stocking Updates: 510 Rainbow Trout, 9.9 inches, stocked 04-13-2026; 490 Rainbow Trout, 9.6 inches, stocked 04-25-2025; 492 Rainbow Trout, 9.6 inches, stocked 03-22-2024; 500 Rainbow Trout, 9.8 inches, stocked 03-24-2023.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: Wilbur D. May Center — Wikipedia (coordinate reference for the arboretum/pond area of Rancho San Rafael Park; no dedicated USGS GNIS gazetteer record exists for Rancho San Rafael Pond itself)

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Rancho San Rafael Pond is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Rancho San Rafael Pond in NDOW's current regulation table; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Rancho San Rafael Pond)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Rancho San Rafael Pond.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; no fixed clock times or sunrise/sunset offsets are specified for Rancho San Rafael Pond (within Rancho San Rafael Regional Park).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Rancho San Rafael Pond is allowed during the hours the park is open to the public.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours parks are open to public use

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Rancho San Rafael Pond.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Rancho San Rafael Pond; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
